architecture_fit.note: Bilinear q*k routes to the largest-index opener (nearest/recent-opener heuristic), the exact cheap baseline the goal warns against; not a delta from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Only the benchmark's uniform floor is beaten; no strawman run, and the method itself IS the nearest-opener strawman, so no 'fails-where-mine-succeeds' contrast.

faithfulness.note: No model, no ablation/patching; queries are built from oracle parser-stack ground truth, and the README's matching claim is demonstrably false.

operating_range.note: Does sweep depth 1-5 and shows monotonic decay, but the decay is the broken mechanism degrading, not graceful range; <2 orders of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Circuit is hand-set (no training), but it cheats via oracle stack info and the bilinear form does not reproduce true stack-matching.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails boot-check: gr.empty() AttributeError at import, numpy never imported, demo.load outputs mismatch the dict return.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this viz' describes a crisp-diagonal heatmap, but that diagonal would not appear and the viz does not run.

notes: The hand-built head claims to route closers to their true stack-matching opener, but the bilinear q*k score (q=match index, k=opener position) is monotone in opener position, so argmax lands on the most-recent opener — exactly the positional heuristic the goal asks to rule out. Verified empirically: a closer matching opener 1 with a later nested opener routes to that later opener; depth-1 accuracy is 1.0 only because match==nearest there, and accuracy decays with depth as the heuristic diverges from the stack. It also fabricates queries from oracle parser-stack ground truth (no model, no causal test) and the Gradio app does not boot (gr.empty, missing numpy import).
